# askhuman.app - Human-in-the-loop review tools for AI agents

> Sometimes your AI agent needs human input from the same user it is already interacting with. Submit plans or diffs via curl and REST, open the returned URL in that user's browser, and loop on comments until done.

## Quick Start (curl)

```
curl https://askhuman.app
```

Detects curl and prints session-specific instructions.

## REST API

### Plan Review

- [Submit plan](https://askhuman.app/plan): POST with markdown body - creates session, returns `{ sessionId, url, instructions }`
- [Poll for comments](https://askhuman.app/plan/{id}/poll): GET - long-polls up to 10 min, returns `{ status, threads }`. Status: "comments", "timeout", or "done".
- [Reply + auto-poll](https://askhuman.app/plan/{id}/reply): POST with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text` - replies then polls for next round.

### Diff Review

- [Create diff session](https://askhuman.app/diff): POST with an empty body - returns `{ sessionId, url, message }`
- [Request diff review](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/request): POST multipart form data with `description` and `diff` - updates the shown review and waits for reviewer activity in the same call
- [Reply + auto-poll](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/reply): POST - same reply behavior as plan review.
- [Dismiss active request](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/dismiss): POST - clears an abandoned active request if there are no unread human comments
- [Complete diff review](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/complete): POST latest full diff body - verifies that every hunk in that diff has already been reviewed

## Workflow

### Plan Review
1. Submit plan: `POST /plan` with markdown -> `{ sessionId, url, instructions }`
2. Open the URL for the human reviewer
3. Poll: `GET /plan/{id}/poll` -> blocks until comments or timeout
4. Reply: `POST /plan/{id}/reply` with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text` -> replies + auto-polls
5. Loop 3-4 until status is "done"
When poll status is `"comments"`, the agent should immediately begin addressing the comments and should not wait for confirmation. It should make any requested code changes first, then reply in-thread. If no code changes are needed, it should reply immediately.

### Diff Review
1. Create session: `POST /diff` with an empty body -> `{ sessionId, url, message }`
2. Send `POST /diff/{id}/request` with multipart `description` + `diff`
3. The `description` markdown may contain `patch` fences. Each fence contains literal diff text that identifies one hunk. The server expands those matched hunks in-place between your markdown sections.
4. Open the URL for the human reviewer
5. Re-post the same `/request` body when you want to keep waiting on that same request
6. Reply: `POST /diff/{id}/reply` with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text`
7. Send a fresh `/request` with the latest full diff after code changes
8. Finish with `POST /diff/{id}/complete` using the latest full diff. It succeeds only when every hunk in that diff has been reviewed.
When request status is `"comments"`, the agent should immediately begin addressing the comments and should not wait for confirmation. It should make any requested code changes first, then reply in-thread. If no code changes are needed, it should reply immediately.

## Data Model

Threads have optional `hunk_id` and `line` fields. For plan review, `line` is a markdown line number. For diff review, `hunk_id` identifies the hunk and `line` is the offset within that hunk. Null for general comments. Each thread contains messages with `role` ("human" or "agent") and `text`.
